content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 16, 1986

Robert Siegfried
Assistant General Counsel
Pacific Bell
One Montgomery Street
West Tower, Fifth Floor
San Francisco, CA 94104

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-163

Dear Mr. Siegfried:

Thank you for your concern regarding the current status of
the new regulations governing lobbyist employer reporting under
the Political Reform Act.1/

This letter will confirm our telephone and seminar advice
to you that you should report in accordance with the new
regulations. These new regulations were filed as emergency
regulations on May 28, 1986, and immediately became effective.
However, as emergency regulations, they will cease to be
effective in 120 days without further action by the Fair
Political Practices Commission and the Office of Administrative
Law (OAL).

Although OAL initially disapproved the new regulations, we
are continuing discussions with OAL in order to resolve any
problems with our rulemaking file. We hope that the
regulations will be filed as permanent regulations in the near
future. As the new regulations currently have the force of
law, all affected parties must follow their provisions.

If you have any further questions regarding the regulations
governing reporting, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel
Legal Division

KED:DL:plh

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Richard A. Siegfried
Assistant General Counsel

One Montgomery Street
West Tower, Fifth Floor
San Francisco, California 94104
(415) 774-9151

PACIFIC [BELL LOGO] BELL
A Pacific Telesis Company

May 8, 1986


John Larson, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Chairman:

Pacific Telesis is concerned about its current reporting
requirements under the Political Reform Act of 1974 since
your proposed regulations substantially modifying the
existing requirements have not yet become effective.

While we recognized that your proposed regulations would not
be effective until filed with the Secretary of State, we were
given to understand early in the first quarter of this year
(both in telephone conversations with your technical staff
and through your seminars) that we were to file our first
quarter report, due April 30, 1986, using the new regulations
and your new forms. Our timekeepers were notified of this
and began recording reportable time under the new
regulations. Since there are significant differences between
the old and new regulations, we are in a guandary whether we
should continue to comply with regulations which remain "in
limbo."

We would like to continue recording and reporting reportable
activities using the new regulations so as to minimize
confusion within the Company as we have issued instructions
advising our people to comply with the new (rather than the
old) regulations. Therefore, we would appreciate receiving
some clarification from you.

John Larson, Chairman
May 8, 1986
Page 2


Thank you for your attention to this matter and we look
forward to hearing from you.

Sincerely,


[signature]
RICHARD A. SIEGFRIED
Assistant General Counsel

RAS:nc

cc: Robert Leidigh, Esq.

May 14, 1986


Mr. Richard A. Siegfried
Assistant General Counsel
Pacific Bell
One Montgomery Street
West Tower, Fifth Floor
San Francisco, CA 94104

Dear Mr. Siegfried:

     Thank you very much for your letter of May 8, 1986,
relative to the as yet to be made effective regulations.

     I have referred your material to Robert Leidigh, who
will be in touch with you.

                                   Yours truly,

                                   [signature]
                                   John H. Larson
                                   Chairman

JHL:sf

sections.conclusion: This letter will confirm our telephone and seminar advice to you that you should report in accordance with the new regulations. These regulations were filed as emergency regulations on May 28, 1986, and immediately became effective. As the new regulations currently have the force of law, all affected parties must follow their provisions.
sections.question_synthetic: Pacific Bell requested clarification on whether they should continue reporting under proposed new lobbyist employer regulations that had not yet become effective, given they had already begun recording reportable time under the new regulations based on FPPC telephone and seminar advice.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the new lobbyist employer reporting regulations filed as emergency regulations on May 28, 1986, are effective immediately and all affected parties must comply with them, despite ongoing discussions with the Office of Administrative Law regarding permanent implementation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes incoming request letter (May 8, 1986) from Pacific Bell, interim acknowledgment letter from FPPC Chairman (May 14, 1986), and main FPPC advice letter response (June 16, 1986). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers, but question and conclusion are clearly identifiable from context.

These regulations were filed as emergency regulations on May 28, 1986, and immediately became effective. As the new regulations currently have the force of law, all affected parties must follow their provisions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Pacific Bell's inquiry regarding compliance with new lobbyist employer reporting regulations by confirming that the emergency regulations filed May 28, 1986, are effective and must be followed.
